title: One-Shot Bodies, Streaming, Uploads, Decompression, and Resource Limits · Go Client Systems · Contract Triage
summary: An on-call reliability engineer tracing a production client incident must isolate a plausible but incorrect result from conflicting traces, repair its cause, and retain a regression witness for one-shot bodies, streaming, uploads, decompression, and resource limits, producing bounded streaming body pipeline. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in a Go 1.26 net/http bounded streaming body pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress thr…
objectives: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Limit compressed and decoded response reads before allocation, parsing, logging, or persistence. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.

Stream large uploads with known ownership, backpressure, progress, cancellation, and retryability. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go Inspect the failure record · Debug One Shot Body Ownership
steps.instruction:
debug case — Go incident collection agent streams a 12 MiB upload and receives a 40 KiB compressed body that expands past 30 MiB. Define one-shot ownership, replay, compressed and decoded limits, cancellation, trailers, and cleanup. Competency probe: Treat request and response bodies as owned one-shot streams with explicit read, close, replay, and cleanup rules. Demonstrate the result through Go 1.26 net/http, net/url, context, io, crypto/tls, RoundTripper, and httptest evidence.
